risk_if_not_implemented: Without Retention Of Previous Configurations, systems may operate with insecure settings or unauthorized changes, expanding the attack surface.

compensating_control_1.name: Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP)
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to implement Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP), or similar technology, to automatically and securely configure devices upon being added to a network.
compensating_control_1.justification: Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) (CFG-07) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Retention Of Previous Configurations (CFG-02.3) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Configuration Enforcement
compensating_control_2.description: Automated mechanisms exist to monitor, enforce and report on configurations for endpoint devices.
compensating_control_2.justification: Configuration Enforcement (CFG-06) provides configuration hardening that compensates for the absence of Retention Of Previous Configurations (CFG-02.3) by eliminating unnecessary services and enforcing secure settings to reduce inherent exposure. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
